div.p: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted, and by this my charter to have made firm, the friendly agreement made at Selkirk in my presence and in my full court, on the one side between Earl Patrick of Dunbar, with his son and heir, Patrick, consenting, and the house of Melrose on the other side, about the disagreement which arose between them concerning the pasture towards the west of Leader. Wherefore I will and firmly instruct that the said agreement between them made thereupon be inviolably and constantly held fast, just as the cyrograph made between them bears witness. Saving my service. As witnesses, Brice bishop of Moray, Ralph bishop of Down, William and Oliver my chaplains, Robert of London my son, David of Lindsay and Gervase Avenel, justices, William del Bois and Hugh de Sigillo my clerks, Roger de Mortimer, Adam of Kinghorn, John of Maxwell, Alexander of Synton. At Selkirk on the 6th day of November.
